risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "Participants were assigned to the programme or the no-treatment control group directly after the baseline measurement using stratified randomisation. This randomisation was performed in blocks of two on the basis of one prognostic factor: the level of concern about falls (some, regular, often, and very often). Computerised alternative allocation was used in the randomisation process, which was performed by an external agency." Comment: This describes a stratified, block-based randomisation process with computerised allocation performed by an external agency, which is sufficient to indicate a truly random allocation sequence.

_evidence.method_quote: “Participants were assigned to the programme or the no-treatment control group directly after the baseline measurement using stratified randomisation. This randomisation was performed in blocks of two on the basis of one prognostic factor: the level of concern about falls (some, regular, often, and very often). Computerised alternative allocation was used in the randomisation process, which was performed by an external agency.”
_evidence.method_summary: Stratified randomisation in blocks of two using computerised alternative allocation by an external agency.

risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "Computerised alternative allocation was used in the randomisation process, which was performed by an external agency." Comment: This explicitly describes a central allocation process managed by an external agency, which would prevent the recruiter from foreseeing the next assignment before enrolment.

_evidence.concealment_quote: "Computerised alternative allocation was used in the randomisation process, which was performed by an external agency."

risk_of_bias.domain: Blinding of participants and personnel (performance bias)
risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "Data for the effect evaluation is gathered by means of telephone interviews which are conducted by trained interviewers, who are blinded for group allocation." Comment: This states that outcome assessors were blinded, but it does not indicate that participants or the programme facilitators were blinded. The intervention was a behavioural in-home programme delivered face-to-face and by telephone, so knowledge of allocation by participants/personnel was inherent and could plausibly influence the many subjective patient-reported outcomes (e.g. FES-I, avoidance of activity, anxiety, depression, quality of life).

_evidence.blinding_quote: Data for the effect evaluation is gathered by means of telephone interviews which are conducted by trained interviewers, who are blinded for group allocation.
outcome_categories: avoidance of activity owing to concerns about falls (FES-IAB)
no. of times medical attention required as a result of falls
catastrophic beliefs about the consequences of a fall
subjective_outcomes: avoidance of activity owing to concerns about falls (FES-IAB)
catastrophic beliefs about the consequences of a fall
objective_outcomes: no. of times medical attention required as a result of falls

risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "Data for the effect evaluation is gathered by means of telephone interviews which are conducted by trained interviewers, who are blinded for group allocation." Comment: The study explicitly states that the outcome assessors were blinded. The main patient-reported outcomes (e.g. FES-I, FES-IAB, GARS, HADS, SF-12) were collected by blinded interviewers, so knowledge of allocation was unlikely to influence measurement of these outcomes. The fall calendar outcomes are also relatively objective. Overall, this supports low risk of detection bias.

_evidence.assessor_blinding_quote: Data for the effect evaluation is gathered by means of telephone interviews which are conducted by trained interviewers, who are blinded for group allocation.

outcomes.label: avoidance of activity owing to concerns about falls
no. of times medical attention required as a result of falls
perceived consequences of falling - loss of functional independence subscale
perceived consequences of falling - damage to identity subscale
catastrophic beliefs about the consequences of a fall

risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "Data of the effect evaluation will be analysed according to both the intention-to-treat and per-protocol principles." Comment: This is a study design paper and does not report actual post-randomization attrition numbers for the analysed outcomes (no CONSORT flow, no per-arm randomized vs analysed counts, no losses/reasons at the relevant follow-up time points). The mention of ITT/PP analysis plans and the absence of observed attrition data means attrition bias cannot be quantified; per the review guidance this should be judged Unclear rather than High.

_evidence.notes: This is a study design paper; no post-randomisation attrition numbers or CONSORT flow for analysed outcomes are reported. The Analysis section states: 'Data of the effect evaluation will be analysed according to both the intention-to-treat and per-protocol principles.'
